Hallucinations.text: The FBI charged a Philadelphia woman, Keonna Thomas, with attempting to travel overseas to fight for ISIS

She is one of three women arrested this week on terror charges. According to an FBI complaint, Thomas sent numerous social media messages dating back to August 2013, expressing her desire to join ISIS and become a martyr. The messages included quotes such as "If we truly knew the realities... we all would be rushing to join our brothers in the front lines pray ALLAH accept us as shuhada [martyrs]" and "When you're a mujahid [violent jihadi fighter] your death becomes a wedding." The FBI also stated that Thomas purchased an electronic visa to Turkey on March 23, which is known as the easiest place to enter Syria and join ISIS. An ISIS manual advises recruits to buy round-trip tickets to vacation spots such as Spain and then purchase tickets for their real destination once they arrive overseas. Thomas was charged with knowingly attempting to provide material support and resources to a designated foreign terrorist organization and could face up to 15 years in prison.

Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down one of its manufacturing plants due to the discovery of listeria contamination in a serving of ice cream originating from that plant. Public health officials have warned consumers not to eat any Blue Bell-branded products made at the Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, plant, including 3-ounce servings marked with the letters O, P, Q, R, S, or T behind the coding date.

Blue Bell has recalled other products and is shutting down the Broken Arrow facility "out of an abundance of caution" to search for a possible cause of contamination. The CDC advises individuals and institutions to check their freezers for the recalled products and throw them away. This is the first product recall in Blue Bell Creameries' 108-year history. Listeriosis is a serious infection caused by eating food contaminated with listeria, and primarily affects the elderly, pregnant women, newborns, and people with weakened immune systems. Symptoms of a listeria infection include fever and muscle aches, sometimes associated with diarrhea or other gastrointestinal symptoms.

The review found that the failure encompassed reporting, editing, editorial supervision, and fact-checking. The magazine's managing editor, Will Dana, apologized to readers and those damaged by the article, including members of the Phi Kappa Psi fraternity and UVA administrators and students.

Mary Kay Letourneau and her husband, Vili Fualaau, are still happily together despite her jail time for their sexual relationship. They will celebrate their 10th wedding anniversary on May 20. During an exclusive interview with Barbara Walters on ABC's "20/20," Letourneau will discuss what makes their marriage work, her plans to teach again, and her intention to have her status as a registered sex offender lifted. Fualaau will also share his experiences with alcoholism, depression, and why he believes the system failed him while he was still a minor.

B.B. King, the legendary blues musician, was hospitalized for dehydration due to his Type II diabetes. However, his daughter, Claudette King, told the Los Angeles Times that he is now feeling much better and is expected to leave the hospital today. King's publicist, Angela Moore, confirmed that he is back home resting and spending time with his grandchildren.

King has used various models of Gibson guitars over the years, including his signature model Lucille, which was officially dropped by Gibson in the 1980s. Some of his notable hits include "The Thrill Is Gone," "There Must be a Better World Somewhere," and "When Love Comes to Town," a collaboration with U2. Last year, King canceled the remainder of his tour due to dehydration and exhaustion after a show in Chicago.

Getting caught napping on the job in the cargo hold of a plane is never a good idea. Alaska Airlines Flight 448 experienced this firsthand when a ramp agent from Menzies Aviation, a contractor for Alaska Airlines, was found asleep in the cargo hold during a flight from Seattle-Tacoma International Airport to Los Angeles on Monday afternoon. The pilot reported unusual banging sounds coming from the cargo hold,
and temperature-controlled, and the plane was only in the air for 14 minutes.

A federal air marshal quickly intervened and alerted the flight crew, who made an emergency landing in Los Angeles. The ramp agent was taken to an area hospital as a precaution but was later released after passing a drug test. Alaska Airlines is investigating the incident, and all ramp employees undergo full criminal background checks before being hired. The flight with 170 passengers and six crew members on board made it to Los Angeles early Monday evening without any further incidents

Emy Afalava, a loyal American and decorated veteran, was born in American Samoa, a U.S. territory since 1900. Despite being subject to American law his whole life, Afalava has been denied the right to vote because the federal government insists that he is no citizen. This injustice clouds the recent celebration of the 115th anniversary of American Samoa's decision to join the United States. Afalava and other American Samoans are now seeking to right this wrong in a federal lawsuit before the D.C. Circuit. A decision could come any day.

Seven people, including Illinois State University associate men's basketball coach Torrey Ward and deputy athletic director Aaron Leetch, died in a plane crash while returning from the NCAA tournament final. The small plane, a Cessna 414, went down overnight Monday about 2 miles east of the Central Illinois Regional Airport in Bloomington, McLean County Sheriff's Office Sgt. Bill Tate said. The crash occurred about 5 miles from the campus of Illinois State, where Ward and Leetch worked. The National Transportation Safety Board tweeted about the crash. Ward was a well-respected and much-loved colleague in our athletics department, said Illinois State University President Larry Dietz. Words cannot fully express the grief that is felt in the wake of such a tragedy, Dietz added
Ward played professionally in China alongside Yao Ming before getting into coaching

He was part of the staffs at Jacksonville State University, the University of Mississippi, and, for one year, in China before coming to Illinois State as an assistant prior to the 2012 season. He was promoted to associate head coach in May 2014, according to his official bio. Leetch joined ISU in 2005 as an athletic director for development before assuming a number of other roles through 2011, his bio notes.

Loeb is suing Vergara to prevent her from destroying the embryos, which were created in 2013, and wants to implant them in a surrogate and bring them to term. Larry Johnson remembers the fear and feeling of helplessness from being on the SkyWest Airlines flight that made an emergency landing in Buffalo, New York. "I thought we were done," he said Thursday, one day later. "There was no doubt in my mind that we weren't going to land." Johnson was flying with his brother, his girlfriend, and his 8-month-old son when he says a flight attendant came over the speaker asking for someone who was medically trained to help with a sick passenger. Minutes later, Johnson says, the attendant announced there was a pressurization problem

According to Marissa Snow, spokeswoman for SkyWest, three passengers reported a loss of consciousness while on the flight. Fifteen passengers were examined by medical personnel at the Buffalo airport, but no one was transported to the hospital, she said. The spokeswoman said that maintenance personnel found no indication of a pressurization problem with the aircraft, an Embraer E170, and that the airline continues to investigate

Aaron Hernandez, a former New England Patriots tight end, was convicted of first-degree murder and sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. Despite his arrogant demeanor in court, the jury found him guilty of the 2013 murder of Odin Lloyd. Hernandez's life was marked by trouble from a young age, including the sudden death of his father and a series of run-ins with the law. He was drafted by the Patriots in 2009, but his career was cut short when he was charged with murder. The NFL and the Patriots handled the situation properly by releasing him from the team hours after he was charged and not trying to defend him as a "decent, family-loving guy." The case highlights the difficulty of predicting the actions of others, even those with great talent and opportunity. Hernandez himself seemed to accept his fate, telling officers escorting him out of the courtroom, "Hey man, I'm going to miss you guys. ... I don't need any luck any more."

The California Public Utilities Commission has ordered Pacific Gas & Electric Co. to pay a record $1.6 billion penalty for unsafe operation of its gas transmission system, including the pipeline rupture that killed eight people in San Bruno in September 2010. The penalty amounts to forced spending on improving pipeline safety, with $850 million going towards "gas transmission pipeline safety infrastructure improvements" and $50 million towards "other remedies to enhance pipeline safety." The commission's president, Michael Picker, stated that PG&E failed to uphold the public's trust and that the CPUC failed to keep vigilant, resulting in lives lost and homes destroyed. PG&E's chief executive officer, Tony Earley, apologized for the tragic event and stated that the company is working to become the safest energy company in the United States.

If a complete stranger asked me for $100 or offered me an apple in a parking lot without explanation, I would politely decline and ask for clarification on their request. I understand that rejection is a natural part of life, and it's important to approach it with empathy and understanding. By asking for clarification, I can better understand the stranger's intentions and determine the best course of action. Additionally, I would remind myself that rejection is not a reflection of my worth or abilities, and that it's important to maintain a positive attitude and keep moving forward.

As for Jia Jiang's 100 Days of Rejection project, I find it fascinating that he was able to turn his fear of rejection into an opportunity for growth and success. By confronting his fear head-on and embracing rejection as a possibility, he was able to overcome his limitations and achieve his goals. His tips for making rejection work for you, such as not running away after a no and asking why, are valuable insights that can be applied to various areas of life. Overall, Jiang's story is a testament to the power of resilience and determination

Over the last year, more than 25,000 people have fought Ebola infections, resulting in over 10,000 deaths. Despite the intensity of the largest Ebola epidemic in history dying down, dozens are still newly infected each week. The latest World Health Organization Report confirmed a total of 30 new confirmed cases of Ebola were reported in the week of April 5, which is the lowest weekly total since May 2014. However, there are mixed reports on stopping the virus completely, with some countries, such as Liberia and Sierra Leone, having more treatment facilities than demand, while in Guinea, there were reports of 21 unsafe burials and 19 confirmed deaths. These data indicate that though surveillance is improving, unknown chains of transmission could be a source of new infections in the coming weeks.

Understanding Xanax's Mechanism of Action

Xanax belongs to a class of drugs called benzodiazepines, which work by enhancing the effects of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain. This leads to a calming effect, reducing excessive brain activity that can contribute to anxiety.
Xanax is known for its rapid onset of action, providing quick relief for acute anxiety episodes. This makes it a valuable tool in emergency situations or for short-term use.

One of the primary concerns with Xanax is the potential for tolerance development. Over time, the body may become accustomed to the medication, necessitating higher doses for the same effect. This can lead to a cycle of increasing dosage, which poses risks for dependence

This increase is higher than the typical cost-of-living adjustment but lower than last year's record-setting 8.7% increase. The average monthly benefit for retired workers will rise by $59 to $1,907. Social Security benefits are adjusted to account for inflation, which helps retirees maintain their buying power over time. However, rising Medicare Part B premiums may offset some of these increases for retirees. The method used to calculate cost-of-living adjustments has been a subject of debate, with experts suggesting alternatives that better reflect retirees' spending patterns. Additionally, Social Security faces a looming funding shortfall, which could lead to significant benefit cuts if not addressed through adjustments to funding and benefits approved by Congress.

* The order of these bases determines DNA's instructions, or genetic code.

* DNA is a double helix formed by base pairs attached to a sugar-phosphate backbone.
* Nearly every cell in a person's body has the same DNA.
* The structure of the double helix is somewhat like a ladder, with the base pairs forming the ladder's rungs and the sugar and phosphate molecules forming the vertical sidepieces of the ladder.
* An important property of DNA is that it can replicate, or make copies of itself.

American Meaning:

Teresa is the Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian form of Theresa, a name of uncertain etymology.
